title: SSRF bypass via unprotected built-in http/https/net modules in Custom Function sandbox (utils.ts)
description: Flowise runs user-supplied Custom Function JavaScript inside a NodeVM sandbox. SSRF protection is implemented by mocking axios and node-fetch with secure wrappers that enforce the deny list, but the sandbox's allowed built-in module list (defaultAllowBuiltInDep in packages/components/src/utils.ts) includes 'http', 'https', and 'net' (and 'tls'). Because only axios and node-fetch are wrapped, an authenticated user can require('http')/require('https')/require('net') directly and issue raw requests to internal endpoints (e.g. http.request to 169.254.169.254 for AWS IMDS credentials), completely bypassing the HTTP_DENY_LIST-based SSRF controls.
